---
title: "About"
---

The SSDSE Dataset Repository collects datasets that statistics and data science teachers can use for
tests, quizzes, and class activities. Datasets may be real, simulated, or synthetic, and each entry
includes information submitted by the contributor: the source, how clean the data are, how widely
available they are, the number of observations and variables, and the topics the data can be used to
teach or assess.

## How it works

Teachers submit datasets through a [submission form](https://amstat.jotform.com/team/education/ssdse-dataset-repository-submission)
maintained by the American Statistical Association. Submissions land in a
[public Google Sheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1tuCXok_y_zQpSDGssaypETf9lnB6nCh9XVoCiMaPCuc/edit?gid=591469695),
and this site rebuilds itself automatically from that sheet, so new submissions appear here without
any manual work.

## Contributing

Have a dataset you use in your teaching? [Submit it here](https://amstat.jotform.com/team/education/ssdse-dataset-repository-submission).
You will be asked for a brief description, the data file (or a link to it), optional supplementary
material such as a codebook, and information that helps other teachers decide whether the dataset
fits their needs.

## Questions

Some contributors provided an email address and are willing to answer questions about their datasets;
you will find contact information on individual dataset pages where available.
